title: lichen_l2.c: fail_late_init label used for single failure path
description:
Location: lichen_l2.c:468-494

lichen_l2_iface_init() has a goto fail_late_init target, but only one call site uses it (line 469, after lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64 fails). Earlier failures return directly.

What goes wrong: The goto pattern suggests multiple cleanup paths might use this label, but only one does. A reader may scan for other 'goto fail_late_init' uses and find none, wondering if code was deleted or if this is over-engineering for a single error case.

Suggested fix: Either inline the cleanup at the single failure point (it's only ~10 lines), or add a comment at the label: /* Common cleanup for failures after RX callback registration */.
close_reason: Fixed: Added inline comment at goto site explaining purpose (cleanup needed after RX callback registration) and pointing to label for details
